_meta.note: Frozen outputs of lib/terravalue-engine.js across the test fixtures. Future runs assert against these. When intentionally changing engine math, re-run generate-snapshots.js and commit the updated file alongside the change.

carbon.label: Carbon Sequestration
carbon.methodology: 2.6 t CO2/acre/yr × $190/tonne (EPA SC-GHG 2023, 2% near-term discount rate)
carbon.source: Atlanta iTree Eco 2014; EPA Social Cost of Greenhouse Gases 2023 (Table ES-1)

stormwater.label: Stormwater Management
stormwater.methodology: Benefit transfer from USDA CUFR / iTree Eco urban canopy valuation literature
stormwater.source: USDA Center for Urban Forestry Research; iTree Eco model outputs

airQuality.label: Air Quality Improvement
airQuality.methodology: Total pollutant removal value (PM2.5, O3, NO2, SO2) × BenMAP health valuation

energy.label: Energy Savings
energy.source: McPherson 2003; Atlanta iTree Eco; GA Power avg residential rate

habitat.label: Habitat Value
habitat.methodology: Benefit transfer from ecosystem services valuation literature
habitat.source: Troy & Wilson 2006; Brander & Koetse 2011 (approximate, benefit transfer)

propertyPremium.label: Property Value Premium
propertyPremium.methodology: ~7% premium for mature canopy coverage (meta-analysis, approximate)
propertyPremium.source: Kovacs et al. 2022; Netusil et al. 2014

healthBenefits.source: Nowak et al. 2014 (air quality via pollutant removal)

methodology.canopyConversion: 1 Soil Score point ≈ 0.5% canopy coverage change
methodology.valueModel: Netusil et al. 2014 (0.17% property value per 1% canopy, 500m buffer)
methodology.diminishingReturns: Cho et al. 2020 (reduced marginal returns above 40% canopy)
methodology.ecoServices: Atlanta iTree Eco 2014 + EPA SC-GHG 2023 ($190/t CO2) + peer-reviewed rates ($2004/canopy-acre)
methodology.marketBaseline: FHFA HPI Atlanta metro long-term avg (3.5% annual)
methodology.disclaimer: Projections use peer-reviewed coefficients with linear interpolation. Actual results depend on species, placement, maturity, soil conditions, microclimate, and market factors. This is a research-backed directional estimate.

hvac.source: McPherson 2003; Akbari et al. 2001; GA Power / Atlanta Gas Light rates

credits.name: Construction Activity Pollution Prevention
credits.description: Erosion and sedimentation control plan for all construction activities

credits.name: Site Development — Protect or Restore Habitat
credits.description: Conserve existing natural areas and restore damaged areas with native/adapted vegetation

thresholds.option1: Protect/restore ≥ 40% of site (excl. building footprint) with native vegetation
thresholds.option2: Provide $0.40/sqft for off-site habitat protection via accredited land trust

credits.name: Rainwater Management
credits.description: Manage on-site rainwater to reduce runoff volume and improve water quality

credits.name: Heat Island Reduction
credits.description: Minimize effects on microclimates and human/wildlife habitats through canopy/materials

thresholds.nonroof: Tree canopy within 10yr: ≥ 50% hardscape; or SRI ≥ 29 paving; or combination
thresholds.roof: Vegetated roof ≥ 75%; or SRI-compliant ≥ 75%; or combination

credits.name: Light Pollution Reduction
credits.description: Minimize light trespass, reduce sky-glow, and improve nighttime visibility

credits.name: Optimize Energy Performance
credits.description: Reduce energy cost through efficiency measures including passive design

achievableLevel.note: Estimated with 35 points from non-site categories (conservative)

credits.name: Site Selection
credits.description: Encourage use of previously developed and/or contaminated land

credits.name: Ecological Value of Site and Protection
credits.description: Assess and protect existing ecological value of the site

credits.name: Minimising Impact on Existing Site Ecology
credits.description: Minimize negative impact on existing site ecology

credits.name: Enhancement of Ecological Value
credits.description: Enhance the ecological value of the site — 10% Biodiversity Net Gain required (2024)

thresholds.minimum: 10% Biodiversity Net Gain (BNG) — UK law since Feb 2024
thresholds.3credits: Significant net gain with long-term management plan

credits.name: Long-term Biodiversity Management
credits.description: Long-term management and maintenance of site ecology

credits.name: Visual Lighting Design
credits.description: Provide views to nature and daylight access

thresholds.requirement: ≥ 75% of regularly occupied area has views to nature/outdoors

credits.name: Biophilia I — Qualitative
credits.description: Develop a biophilia plan incorporating environmental elements at each design stage

credits.name: Biophilia II — Quantitative
credits.description: Prescribe visual and physical connection with outdoor nature

thresholds.plantWall: Plant wall per floor ≥ 2% of floor area (or largest available wall)
thresholds.pottedPlants: Potted plants or planted beds ≥ 1% of floor area per floor
thresholds.outdoorSpace: Outdoor space with nature accessible within 200m of building entrance

credits.targetValue: Plant wall ≥2% floor area; Potted plants ≥1% floor area

credits.name: Enhanced Air Quality
credits.description: Improve outdoor air quality through green infrastructure

credits.name: Site Selection
credits.description: Encourage selection of previously developed sites, brownfield redevelopment

credits.name: Site Development & Ecological Enhancement
credits.description: Protect and restore natural features, minimize site disturbance

thresholds.erosionControl: Erosion and sediment control during construction
thresholds.nativePlanting: ≥ 50% of landscaped area with native/adapted species
thresholds.canopy: Tree canopy target for site based on climate zone

credits.name: Stormwater Management
credits.description: Manage stormwater through green infrastructure and LID techniques

credits.name: Energy Performance
credits.description: Reduce energy consumption including passive cooling from vegetation

credits.name: Rainwater Management
credits.description: Manage on-site rainwater to reduce runoff volume and improve water quality
actions: Install bioswales, rain gardens, or permeable pavement
Increase canopy to improve rainfall interception (35% rate)

credits.name: Heat Island Reduction
credits.description: Minimize effects on microclimates and human/wildlife habitats through canopy/materials

thresholds.nonroof: Tree canopy within 10yr: ≥ 50% hardscape; or SRI ≥ 29 paving; or combination
thresholds.roof: Vegetated roof ≥ 75%; or SRI-compliant ≥ 75%; or combination

actions: Increase canopy coverage to shade ≥50% of hardscape
Consider strategic tree planting on south and west exposures

warnings: Sales Comparison uses synthetic comparables — not real transactions
Income approach uses estimated GPI (6.5% of market value) — not actual rental data

valuation.methodology: Three-approach reconciliation per USPAP Standard 1-6. Weights assigned by property type and data quality, consistent with JLL Valuation Advisory and Appraisal Institute (MAI) practice.
valuation.disclaimer: This analysis is a research-backed estimate using institutional methodology. It does not constitute a certified appraisal under USPAP. For lending, litigation, or tax purposes, engage a licensed appraiser (MAI/SRA).

adjustedComparables.address: Comparable 1 — Larger lot, slightly less canopy
Comparable 2 — Smaller lot, newer, more canopy
Comparable 3 — Similar property, slight size difference
Comparable 4 — Smaller lot, newer build, premium canopy

salesComparison.methodology: USPAP-compliant paired sales analysis with market-derived adjustments. Canopy premium per Netusil et al. 2014. Time adjustments per FHFA HPI (long-term avg).
salesComparison.syntheticDisclaimer: Comparables were generated from subject property data for demonstration purposes. These are NOT real transactions. For accurate valuation, provide actual comparable sales.

capRateRange.source: Estimated range based on CBRE/JLL survey data

incomeCapitalization.methodology: USPAP Standards 1 & 2. Direct cap per Appraisal Institute methodology. DCF with explicit cash flow projection, market-derived discount rates, and terminal cap reversion. Cap rate benchmarks from JLL/CBRE 2024 surveys.
incomeCapitalization.incomeDisclaimer: Gross potential income was estimated from market value (6.5% GRM). For accurate income analysis, provide actual rental income data.

costApproach.methodology: USPAP-compliant cost approach. RCN from RS Means 2024 Southeast. Age-life depreciation per Marshall Valuation Service. Ecosystem land premium per Netusil/Kovacs (P&X TerraValue methodology).

usesAnalyzed.note: Value derived from capitalized ecosystem services

ecosystemConsideration.note: Current canopy generates ~$207/yr in ecosystem services (capitalized value: $4,140). HBU analysis should weigh this against development returns.

highestAndBestUse.conclusion: Highest and best use as single-family residential. Current use may not represent HBU.
highestAndBestUse.methodology: Four-test HBU analysis per Appraisal Institute standards. Residual land value method for feasibility. Ecosystem services capitalized at 5% discount rate (P&X methodology).

carbon.label: Carbon Sequestration
carbon.methodology: 2.6 t CO2/acre/yr × $190/tonne (EPA SC-GHG 2023, 2% near-term discount rate)
carbon.source: Atlanta iTree Eco 2014; EPA Social Cost of Greenhouse Gases 2023 (Table ES-1)

stormwater.label: Stormwater Management
stormwater.methodology: Benefit transfer from USDA CUFR / iTree Eco urban canopy valuation literature
stormwater.source: USDA Center for Urban Forestry Research; iTree Eco model outputs

airQuality.label: Air Quality Improvement
airQuality.methodology: Total pollutant removal value (PM2.5, O3, NO2, SO2) × BenMAP health valuation

energy.source: McPherson 2003; Atlanta iTree Eco; GA Power avg residential rate

habitat.label: Habitat Value
habitat.methodology: Benefit transfer from ecosystem services valuation literature
habitat.source: Troy & Wilson 2006; Brander & Koetse 2011 (approximate, benefit transfer)

propertyPremium.label: Property Value Premium
propertyPremium.methodology: ~7% premium for mature canopy coverage (meta-analysis, approximate)
propertyPremium.source: Kovacs et al. 2022; Netusil et al. 2014

methodology.framework: Three-approach USPAP-compliant valuation with ecosystem overlay
methodology.ecosystemIntegration: P&X TerraValue engine — peer-reviewed canopy premium coefficients
sources: Appraisal Institute — The Appraisal of Real Estate, 15th Ed.
USPAP 2024-2025 Edition (The Appraisal Foundation)
JLL Valuation Advisory — Cap Rate Survey 2024
CBRE North America Cap Rate Survey H2 2024
RS Means Building Construction Cost Data 2024 (Southeast)
Marshall Valuation Service — Depreciation Tables
FHFA House Price Index — Atlanta-Sandy Springs-Roswell MSA
Netusil et al. 2014 — Implicit value of tree cover (meta-analysis). DOI: 10.1016/j.ecolecon.2016.04.018
Kovacs et al. 2022 — Tree cover and property values (national)
methodology.disclaimer: This analysis is a research-backed estimate using institutional methodology. It does not constitute a certified appraisal under USPAP. For lending, litigation, or tax purposes, engage a licensed appraiser (MAI/SRA).

CBRE North America Cap Rate Survey H2 2024
RS Means Building Construction Cost Data 2024 (Southeast)
Marshall Valuation Service — Depreciation Tables
FHFA House Price Index — Atlanta-Sandy Springs-Roswell MSA
Netusil et al. 2014 — Implicit value of tree cover (meta-analysis). DOI: 10.1016/j.ecolecon.2016.04.018

methodology.disclaimer: This analysis is a research-backed estimate using institutional methodology. It does not constitute a certified appraisal under USPAP. For lending, litigation, or tax purposes, engage a licensed appraiser (MAI/SRA).

warnings: Sales Comparison uses synthetic comparables — not real transactions
Income approach uses estimated GPI (6.5% of market value) — not actual rental data

valuation.methodology: Three-approach reconciliation per USPAP Standard 1-6. Weights assigned by property type and data quality, consistent with JLL Valuation Advisory and Appraisal Institute (MAI) practice.
valuation.disclaimer: This analysis is a research-backed estimate using institutional methodology. It does not constitute a certified appraisal under USPAP. For lending, litigation, or tax purposes, engage a licensed appraiser (MAI/SRA).

adjustedComparables.address: Comparable 1 — Larger lot, slightly less canopy
Comparable 2 — Smaller lot, newer, more canopy
Comparable 3 — Similar property, slight size difference
Comparable 4 — Smaller lot, newer build, premium canopy

salesComparison.methodology: USPAP-compliant paired sales analysis with market-derived adjustments. Canopy premium per Netusil et al. 2014. Time adjustments per FHFA HPI (long-term avg).
salesComparison.syntheticDisclaimer: Comparables were generated from subject property data for demonstration purposes. These are NOT real transactions. For accurate valuation, provide actual comparable sales.

capRateRange.source: Estimated range based on CBRE/JLL survey data

incomeCapitalization.methodology: USPAP Standards 1 & 2. Direct cap per Appraisal Institute methodology. DCF with explicit cash flow projection, market-derived discount rates, and terminal cap reversion. Cap rate benchmarks from JLL/CBRE 2024 surveys.
incomeCapitalization.incomeDisclaimer: Gross potential income was estimated from market value (6.5% GRM). For accurate income analysis, provide actual rental income data.

costApproach.methodology: USPAP-compliant cost approach. RCN from RS Means 2024 Southeast. Age-life depreciation per Marshall Valuation Service. Ecosystem land premium per Netusil/Kovacs (P&X TerraValue methodology).

usesAnalyzed.note: Value derived from capitalized ecosystem services

ecosystemConsideration.note: Current canopy generates ~$1,603/yr in ecosystem services (capitalized value: $32,060). HBU analysis should weigh this against development returns.

highestAndBestUse.conclusion: Highest and best use as office. Current use may not represent HBU.
highestAndBestUse.methodology: Four-test HBU analysis per Appraisal Institute standards. Residual land value method for feasibility. Ecosystem services capitalized at 5% discount rate (P&X methodology).

carbon.label: Carbon Sequestration
carbon.methodology: 2.6 t CO2/acre/yr × $190/tonne (EPA SC-GHG 2023, 2% near-term discount rate)
carbon.source: Atlanta iTree Eco 2014; EPA Social Cost of Greenhouse Gases 2023 (Table ES-1)

stormwater.label: Stormwater Management
stormwater.methodology: Benefit transfer from USDA CUFR / iTree Eco urban canopy valuation literature
stormwater.source: USDA Center for Urban Forestry Research; iTree Eco model outputs

airQuality.label: Air Quality Improvement
airQuality.methodology: Total pollutant removal value (PM2.5, O3, NO2, SO2) × BenMAP health valuation

energy.label: Energy Savings
energy.source: McPherson 2003; Atlanta iTree Eco; GA Power avg residential rate

habitat.label: Habitat Value
habitat.methodology: Benefit transfer from ecosystem services valuation literature
habitat.source: Troy & Wilson 2006; Brander & Koetse 2011 (approximate, benefit transfer)

propertyPremium.label: Property Value Premium
propertyPremium.methodology: ~7% premium for mature canopy coverage (meta-analysis, approximate)
propertyPremium.source: Kovacs et al. 2022; Netusil et al. 2014

methodology.framework: Three-approach USPAP-compliant valuation with ecosystem overlay
methodology.ecosystemIntegration: P&X TerraValue engine — peer-reviewed canopy premium coefficients
sources: Appraisal Institute — The Appraisal of Real Estate, 15th Ed.
